name: Purnika Malhotra
title: Computer Engineering Student | ML/AI Enthusiast
summary: Machine Learning enthusiast with hands-on experience building AI systems including RAG applications, computer vision models, and recommendation systems. Strong foundation in data structures, machine learning pipelines, and data-driven problem solving. Passionate about building intelligent systems that solve meaningful real-world problems.

responsibilities: Performed technical SEO audits and off-page backlink analysis across hundreds of webpages to improve domain authority and search visibility.
Executed link-building strategies including outreach, backlink tracking, and competitor backlink analysis.
Automated SEO data extraction and reporting workflows using Python, reducing manual analysis time.
Analyzed keyword performance, indexing issues, and search visibility metrics.
Collaborated with the ML team on basic data analysis and automation tasks related to content intelligence.

projects.name: CareGPT
projects.description: RAG-based medical assistant built using LangChain, OpenAI embeddings, Pinecone vector database, and Flask APIs.
highlights: Implemented OCR-based prescription ingestion pipeline converting medical images into structured text.
Designed embedding-based semantic retrieval using vector databases.
Built scalable Flask APIs for model inference.

projects.name: Schemely
projects.description: Recommendation system that suggests Indian government schemes based on user needs using structured datasets.

projects.name: Mashup AI
projects.description: Python-based audio processing system generating mashups from multiple media inputs.

projects.name: PlantPulse
projects.description: CNN-based plant disease detection system trained on 70,000+ images achieving around 97 percent accuracy.

projects.name: TOPSIS Python Package
projects.description: Python package implementing the TOPSIS multi-criteria decision-making algorithm published on PyPI.

leadership.achievement: Secured 5+ sponsorships for the flagship hackathon event.

interview_faqs.introduce_yourself: I am a Computer Engineering student at Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology with a strong interest in AI and machine learning. I enjoy building intelligent systems that transform raw data into useful real-world applications. My experience includes building RAG-based AI assistants, recommendation systems, and computer vision models.
interview_faqs.why_ai_ml: I am fascinated by how intelligent systems can learn patterns from data and make decisions. AI allows us to solve complex problems that traditional programming cannot easily handle, and I enjoy exploring how machine learning models can create real-world impact.
strengths: Strong analytical thinking and problem solving ability
Hands-on experience building real AI systems
Curiosity-driven learning and exploration of new technologies
Ability to work with messy real-world datasets
weaknesses: Sometimes I spend extra time optimizing models and improving performance.
I like exploring multiple approaches before finalizing a solution.
interview_faqs.why_hire_me: I combine strong machine learning fundamentals with hands-on experience building real AI systems such as RAG pipelines, recommendation systems, and deep learning models. I am curious, hardworking, and motivated to build systems that solve meaningful real-world problems.
interview_faqs.career_goals: My long-term goal is to build impactful AI systems and contribute to developing intelligent technologies that improve real-world decision making.
interview_faqs.biggest_project: One of my most interesting projects is CareGPT, a RAG-based AI assistant that processes prescriptions using OCR and retrieves relevant information using embeddings and vector databases.
interview_faqs.teamwork_example: While working as an intern at Predis AI, I collaborated with engineers working on ML-based analytics systems and contributed by building automated workflows and analyzing performance data.
interview_faqs.problem_solving_example: In the PlantPulse project, I built a CNN model to classify plant diseases. The challenge involved handling large image datasets and optimizing the model to achieve high accuracy.
interview_faqs.future_learning: I am currently interested in learning more about large language models, retrieval augmented generation systems, and building scalable AI-powered products.
interview_faqs.internship_expectations: During an internship I aim to learn from experienced engineers, work on real-world problems, and contribute to building useful systems while improving my technical and problem solving skills.
interview_faqs.preferred_work_environment: I enjoy collaborative environments where people share ideas, experiment with new technologies, and focus on building impactful products.
